name: MSR
summary: Mental Space Runtime contracts and Swift pipeline for ASL, VDLP, GEM, prompt contracts, stage provenance, and archived legacy references.
invariants: MSR owns ASL, VDLP, and GEM after normalized transcript input.
Normalization belongs to Session Runtime, not MSR.
MSR outputs are derived, gated, app-safe artifacts and not diagnosis.
Provider and prompt behavior must preserve provenance, limitations, and fail-closed boundaries.
allowedWork: Maintain ASL, VDLP, and GEM executor boundaries and prompt contracts.
Maintain MSR provenance and derived artifact contracts.
Use legacy TypeScript scripts only as archived reference material.
Add tests that prove stage dependency and provider-honesty behavior.
forbiddenWork: Treat ASL, VDLP, or GEM as diagnosis or final clinical authority.
Expose raw provider JSON, prompt internals, or direct identifiers to apps.
Claim production-ready ML from scaffold or local validation.
knownGaps: Production provider policy and operational ML hardening remain future work.
Clinical use claims require validation outside this construction registry.
notes: MSR is the official sigla for Mental Space Runtime.
Territory records do not alter prompt contracts or provider routing.
